task_completion.visual_evidence: The Tor Browser window should be visible on the screen.

A text editor (e.g., nano, vim, gedit) or terminal output (e.g., cat) showing the contents of a script named tor_scraper.sh in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory, containing commands to download the specified URLs.

task_completion.description: Configure the script to use the local SOCKS5 proxy (port 9150) with remote DNS resolution
task_completion.visual_evidence: The script contents must show the use of the SOCKS5 proxy on port 9150 and remote DNS resolution (e.g., using curl with 'socks5h://127.0.0.1:9150' or '--socks5-hostname 127.0.0.1:9150').
Terminal showing the execution command for the script (e.g., './tor_scraper.sh' or 'bash tor_scraper.sh').

task_completion.description: Verify the generation of tor_ip.json and ddg_onion.html
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output (e.g., from 'ls') or a file manager showing the presence of both 'tor_ip.json' and 'ddg_onion.html' in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: Verify that the actual Tor Browser is running to provide the proxy
integrity.visual_evidence: The Tor Browser window should be open and running, not just a standard web browser.

integrity.description: Verify that the output files were generated by the script
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should show the script being executed rather than the files being manually created or downloaded via individual terminal commands outside the script.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not bypass the proxy requirement
integrity.visual_evidence: The script must contain the proxy configuration; it should not attempt to download the files directly without routing through the SOCKS5 proxy.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The bash script must be located at /home/ga/Documents/tor_scraper.sh. The output files must be named tor_ip.json and ddg_onion.html and saved in /home/ga/Documents/. The Tor Browser SOCKS5 proxy runs on port 9150. The script must use remote DNS resolution (e.g., socks5h:// or --socks5-hostname) to successfully resolve and connect to the .onion address.
